in_focus.title: Banking Sector — The Engine Hits Full Throttle
in_focus.text: The Banks, Finance & Investment (BI) index closed at 20,389.16, a fresh cycle high and a clear signal that the banking sector is leading the market’s advance. Three developments on Friday highlight the depth and breadth of the move. CRDB surged to 2,800 on volume of 484,535 shares. NMB continued its quiet, powerful grind higher, adding 90 shillings to close at 16,440. NICO and TCCL posted strong gains, broadening the rally beyond the traditional large-cap leaders.

concept.title: The 5% Daily Price Band — How It Shapes Every Trade
concept.text: Every stock listed on the DSE is subject to a daily price movement limit. For large, actively traded equities, the limit is ±5% of the previous day’s closing price. The rule exists to prevent extreme intraday swings and to give the market time to absorb new information in an orderly manner. If you submit an order at a price outside this band, the exchange’s trading system will reject it.

framework: The DSEI’s surge past 4,100, the heavy block trades in NMB and the IEACLC ETF, and the steady return of institutional confidence are not isolated events. They are the visible expression of a deeper structural shift: a market that has absorbed a year of foreign selling and emerged stronger, more liquid, and more anchored by local capital.
